T0051.title: Slow Company Flip
T0051.definition: A loan fraud scheme specifically targeting business lending, where fraudsters legally transfer ownership of a company to the loan applicant, with the change properly recorded in business registries. The company’s operating data, invoicing, and tax records are then fabricated to meet bank requirements for legal representative tenure before applying for large business loans. This method is known as a "slow company" in contrast to a "fast company." Typically, after the ownership transfer, the fraudsters inflate business cash flows and maintain normal invoicing and tax payments for 3–6 months before applying for large corporate tax-invoice loans.
